feedback.text: Hello, I strongly support the goals of the Digital Fairness Act and would like to highlight the need for stronger consumer rights regarding the ownership of digital products. Currently, most digital purchases (such as video games, software, and media) are sold as licenses rather than owned goods, allowing companies to revoke access at any time. This undermines consumer protection and erodes trust in the digital marketplace. The recent Stop Killing Games campaign demonstrates how digital products, including fully paid games, can be made inaccessible without warningeven when there are no legal or security reasons to do so. Consumers are left without recourse, despite having paid for what they believed was a permanent product. I urge the Commission to consider a clause ensuring that when consumers purchase digital products, they receive permanent access, not a revocable licenseexcept in clearly defined legal or contractual breach cases. Digital fairness must include digital ownership. Consumers should have the same rights with digital purchases as they do with physical ones.
